topic: Codex Plugins 2026: Guide to 90+ Integrations (Atlassian, GitLab, CodeRabbit)

key_points: Deep dive into how MCP sits at the center of the Codex plugin marketplace
Plugins bundle skills, app integrations, and MCP server configs into installable packages
Agents discover and call external tools at runtime with no code changes
Practical walkthrough of plugin chain: Jira → Codex → CodeRabbit → Render
Comprehensive coverage of April 2026 desktop update with 90+ plugins
Details on Computer Use, in-app browser, and memory features alongside plugins
Named integrations: Atlassian Rovo, CircleCI, CodeRabbit, GitLab Issues, Microsoft Suite
Covers rollout schedule: macOS-first, EU/UK later
Curated list of best Codex plugins for developer productivity
Plugin categories: DevOps, project management, code review, cloud deployment
Step-by-step setup instructions for individual plugins
Productivity benchmarks for teams using plugins vs. without
Enterprise-focused guide to Codex plugin governance
JSON policy files for controlling plugin availability (INSTALLED_BY_DEFAULT, AVAILABLE, NOT_AVAILABLE)
Authentication on install vs. first-use distinction
Data isolation and audit trail requirements for compliance
First-person account from CodeRabbit team on building Codex plugin
CodeRabbit MCP server now lets Codex trigger reviews and generate reports
CodeRabbit consumes context from Datadog, New Relic, SonarQube, Snyk, Grafana before reviewing
Automated PR discussion and review-quality context integration

stats.fact: OpenAI shipped 90+ new plugins in the April 16, 2026 Codex Desktop update, including Atlassian Rovo, CircleCI, CodeRabbit, GitLab Issues, and Microsoft Suite
Codex now serves more than 4 million weekly developers, up from 3 million two weeks earlier, with usage growing 6x since January 2026 and 10x since August 2025
Codex achieves 90% adoption rate among Fortune 100 companies as of 2026
Developers using Codex-style assistants complete tasks 55% faster with approximately 50% quicker time-to-merge
Cisco deployed Codex across its entire engineering organization, achieving 50% reduction in code review times and compressing project timelines from weeks to days
Atlassian Rovo MCP Server reached general availability in February 2026, exposing 60+ tools spanning Jira, Confluence, Bitbucket Cloud, Compass, and Jira Service Management
OpenAI launched the initial plugin marketplace on March 27, 2026 with 20+ plugins; the ecosystem grew to 40+ community contributions within one month
Community-curated list of 150+ Codex ecosystem tools spans 20 categories including subagents, skills, plugins, and MCP servers
76% of developers use or plan to use AI tools in their development process as of 2024 Stack Overflow survey (65,000 developers across 185 countries)
GPT-5.2-Codex is the fastest adopted model OpenAI has ever released, with usage 20x higher than August 2025

angles: Plugin chain automation: how a single natural-language prompt can chain Jira → Codex edit → CodeRabbit review → Render deploy
MCP as the universal glue: why the Model Context Protocol unlocks runtime tool discovery without code changes
Enterprise governance angle: how JSON policy files let IT teams manage plugin availability and credentials at scale
Atlassian Rovo deep-dive: 60+ tools across Jira, Confluence, Bitbucket, Compass, and JSM via OAuth 2.1
CodeRabbit's dual role: it acts both as a Codex plugin (MCP server) and consumes MCP context from monitoring/observability tools
GitLab parity story: teams not on GitHub now have first-class Codex integration via GitLab Issues plugin
Community ecosystem angle: 150+ tools in 20 categories go far beyond OpenAI's official 90+ count
Security and compliance controls: data isolation, audit trails, and VPC service controls for regulated industries
Practical workflow walkthrough: step-by-step setup for the top 5 most-installed plugins (Atlassian, GitLab, CodeRabbit, CircleCI, Render)
outline: H2: What Are Codex Plugins? (Skills + App Integrations + MCP Servers Bundled)
